edges.description: Disrupted Rab regulation perturbs vesicle-mediated trafficking.
Rab-pathway disruption contributes to impaired brain development.
Endomembrane trafficking defects impair developing neural tissues.
Endomembrane trafficking defects impair anterior-segment and lens development.
TBC1D20-related trafficking defects alter ER-to-lipid droplet biology.
Autophagy impairment is linked to neurodevelopmental and ocular disease biology.
Lipid-droplet and ER trafficking defects may contribute to multisystem disease.
Abnormal neurodevelopment causes delayed milestone acquisition.
Abnormal neurodevelopment causes severe cognitive impairment.
Abnormal neurodevelopment causes reduced head circumference.
Abnormal brain development causes corpus callosum hypoplasia.
Ocular developmental defects include small globe size.
Lens opacity is a defining congenital ocular phenotype.
Optic nerve involvement contributes to severe visual impairment.

nodes.description: Early cataract extraction can be used to treat congenital cataracts, though visual outcomes may remain limited by optic nerve and broader neurodevelopmental involvement.

evidence.reference_title: Congenital cataract: a guide to genetic and clinical management
evidence.snippet: Early surgery and close follow up in ophthalmology is important to optimise visual potential and prevent amblyopia.

nodes.description: Congenital cataract is a defining ocular manifestation.
TBC1D20 and RAB3GAP1/Rab-pathway dysfunction links Warburg micro syndrome to impaired autophagy and autophagosome maturation, particularly in nervous system and patient-derived cellular contexts.

evidence.snippet: Recently identified monogenic disorders linking selective autophagy, vesicular trafficking, and other pathways have further expanded the molecular and phenotypical spectrum of congenital disorders of autophagy as a clinical disease spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: TBC1D20 mediates autophagy as a key regulator of autophagosome maturation
evidence.snippet: TBC1D20 mediates autophagy as a key regulator of autophagosome maturation

nodes.description: Affected children have profound global developmental delay.
Agenesis or hypoplasia of the corpus callosum is part of the brain-malformation spectrum.
Warburg micro syndrome genes, particularly TBC1D20 and RAB18/Rab-pathway biology, intersect with ER-to-lipid droplet trafficking and lipid droplet growth.

evidence.reference_title: Rab1b facilitates lipid droplet growth by ER-to-lipid droplet targeting of DGAT2
evidence.snippet: Last, alterations in LD metabolism and DGAT2 redistribution, consistent with Rab1b activity, were caused by mutations in the Rab1b–GTPase activating protein TBC1D20 in Warburg Micro syndrome (WARBM) model mice fibroblasts.

nodes.description: Loss of RAB18 or TBC1D20 function disrupts Rab18-associated membrane trafficking across the endomembrane system, linking Warburg micro syndrome to abnormal vesicular transport and organelle organization.

biological_processes: endoplasmic reticulum to Golgi vesicle-mediated transport

evidence.reference_title: TBC1D20 coordinates vesicle transport and actin remodeling to regulate ciliogenesis
evidence.snippet: Here, we identify TBC1D20 as a novel Rab11 GTPase-activating protein that coordinates vesicle transport and actin remodeling to regulate ciliogenesis.

nodes.description: Reduced head circumference is a core cranial-neurodevelopmental feature.
Microphthalmia is one of the defining ocular malformations.
The convergent nervous-system outcome is severe neurodevelopmental impairment with microcephaly, brain malformations, intellectual disability, abnormal tone, and delayed motor development.

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing identifies a novel pathogenic variant in RAB3GAP1 causing Warburg Micro syndrome in a Pakistani family
evidence.snippet: Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) analysis revealed pronounced cerebral atrophy including corpus callosum hypoplasia and polymicrogyria.

nodes.description: Warburg micro syndrome includes congenital maldevelopment of the eye, especially microphthalmia, microcornea, cataract, and optic nerve atrophy.

evidence.reference_title: A novel mouse model of Warburg Micro Syndrome reveals roles for RAB18 in eye development and organisation of the neuronal cytoskeleton
evidence.snippet: Rab18 mutant mice are viable and fertile. They present with congenital nuclear cataracts and atonic pupils, recapitulating characteristic ocular features associated with WARBM.

nodes.description: Optic nerve atrophy contributes to severe visual impairment.
Polymicrogyria is a cortical malformation reported in WARBM1.

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing identifies a novel pathogenic variant in RAB3GAP1 causing Warburg Micro syndrome in a Pakistani family
evidence.snippet: Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) analysis revealed pronounced cerebral atrophy including corpus callosum hypoplasia and polymicrogyria.

meta.association: Causal biallelic pathogenic variants in RAB18

evidence.reference_title: Warburg Micro syndrome is caused by RAB18 deficiency or dysregulation
evidence.snippet: RAB18 , RAB3GAP1 , RAB3GAP2 and TBC1D20 are each mutated in Warburg Micro syndrome, a rare autosomal recessive multisystem disorder.
RAB18 | HGNC:14244 | Warburg micro syndrome | MONDO:0016649 | AR | Moderate

meta.association: Causal biallelic pathogenic variants in RAB3GAP1

evidence.reference_title: From cataract to syndrome diagnosis: Revaluation of Warburg-Micro syndrome Type 1 patients
evidence.snippet: Although WARBM shows genetic heterogeneity, the pathogenic variants in RAB3GAP1 were the most common cause of WARBM.
RAB3GAP1 | HGNC:17063 | Warburg micro syndrome | MONDO:0016649 | AR | Definitive

meta.association: Causal biallelic pathogenic variants in RAB3GAP2

evidence.reference_title: Warburg Micro syndrome is caused by RAB18 deficiency or dysregulation
evidence.snippet: RAB18 , RAB3GAP1 , RAB3GAP2 and TBC1D20 are each mutated in Warburg Micro syndrome, a rare autosomal recessive multisystem disorder.
RAB3GAP2 | HGNC:17168 | Warburg micro syndrome | MONDO:0016649 | AR | Definitive

nodes.description: Biallelic pathogenic variants in RAB3GAP1 or RAB3GAP2 disrupt the RAB3GAP complex, disturbing Rab-family small GTPase signaling during nervous-system and ocular development.

evidence.reference_title: Warburg Micro syndrome is caused by RAB18 deficiency or dysregulation
evidence.snippet: RAB18 , RAB3GAP1 , RAB3GAP2 and TBC1D20 are each mutated in Warburg Micro syndrome, a rare autosomal recessive multisystem disorder.

nodes.description: Severe intellectual disability is a defining neurodevelopmental manifestation.

meta.association: Causal biallelic pathogenic variants in TBC1D20

evidence.reference_title: Warburg Micro syndrome is caused by RAB18 deficiency or dysregulation
evidence.snippet: RAB18 , RAB3GAP1 , RAB3GAP2 and TBC1D20 are each mutated in Warburg Micro syndrome, a rare autosomal recessive multisystem disorder.
